pages.text: Openings — the doors, the windows, and the things that dress them. $(italic)Macaw's Doors$(), $(italic)Macaw's Trapdoors$(), $(italic)Macaw's Windows$(), and $(italic)Another Furniture$()'s curtains and shutters all gather here.
pages.title: Doors & Windows

pages.text: $(bold)Doors and trapdoors.$() Roughly thirty new $(bold)door$() styles — barn doors that split horizontally, Dutch doors with an upper latch, glass-paned doors, jail doors of iron bars, paper-screen and shoji doors, ornate carved ones. Trapdoors in the same scale on the floor.$(br2)Mechanically all are vanilla doors: the same hinges, the same logic. Stack a Macaw's trapdoor under a Macaw's door for a coherent style.
pages.title: Doors & Trapdoors

pages.text: $(bold)Windows.$() Window frames in every wood; mosaic stained-glass windows in every dye; mullioned panels; sills and sashes. Build them into walls for instant light and character.$(br2)$(italic)Two kinds of shutters exist in the pack$(): Macaw's shutters that operate independently of the window itself, and $(italic)Another Furniture$()'s wooden shutters in every wood (they swing on a hinge). Pick whichever style fits the build.
pages.title: Windows

pages.text: $(bold)Curtains.$() $(italic)Another Furniture$() — dyed cloth curtains, sized for a 1×2 window. Right-click to draw them open or closed. The simplest way to make a window feel inhabited.
pages.title: Curtains
